reasoning: I came to this booth thinking I'd vote BJP again—they're still better than TMC, right? But honestly, I can't anymore. I spent five years convinced I was voting for competence and governance, but watching the pattern these past months—the ED raids timed for elections, the court judgment on SSC jobs right before voting, CAA delivering fourteen certificates when there are lakhs waiting—I finally see what I was doing. I wasn't being rational about policy. I was being played. Both sides are playing the same game, just with different faces. And CAA? It was always the symbol, never the reality. I'm tired. I have a daughter to think about, jobs to worry about, schools to fix. Neither party is actually addressing that. They're just fighting over vote banks and using our trauma as political scoreboard. I came here to vote, but I'm voting NOTA because I can't pretend anymore that I'm choosing competence when all I'm really doing is picking a side in a game that doesn't care about my family.
primary_drivers: Loss of faith in 'competence' narrative after seeing institutional weaponization and political timing
CAA's complete failure to deliver (14 certificates after 4+ years)
Recognition that both parties treat communities as vote banks, not real constituencies
